plants.name: Wheat
plants.description: Staple cereal crop, cool-season grain grown worldwide.
care_tips: Avoid waterlogging
Apply nitrogen fertilizer at tillering stage
Monitor for rust fungus in humid conditions

plants.name: Rice
plants.description: Staple food for half the world, requires flooded or very moist conditions.
care_tips: Maintain standing water 2-5cm during vegetative stage
Drain field 2 weeks before harvest
High nitrogen requirement

plants.name: Corn / Maize
plants.description: High-yield warm-season cereal, very responsive to nitrogen.
care_tips: Critical irrigation period: silking and tasseling
Deep root system tolerates short droughts
Heavy feeder — apply NPK in split doses

plants.name: Barley
plants.description: Hardy cool-season grain, tolerates drought better than wheat.
care_tips: Sensitive to waterlogging — ensure good drainage
Tolerates slightly alkaline soils well
Sow in early spring or autumn

plants.name: Sorghum
plants.description: Drought-tolerant warm-season grain, excellent for hot dry climates like Basra.
care_tips: Drought stomata close efficiently — do not over-irrigate
Excellent for alkaline soils
Ratoon cropping possible after first harvest

plants.name: Tomato
plants.description: High-value warm-season vegetable, very sensitive to temperature extremes.
care_tips: Blossom drop occurs above 32°C — shade cloth in summer
Calcium deficiency causes blossom end rot
Consistent moisture prevents cracking

plants.name: Potato
plants.description: Cool-season tuber crop, prefers loose well-draining acidic soil.
care_tips: Hill soil around stems as plant grows
Low pH critical to prevent scab disease
Stop irrigation 2 weeks before harvest to harden skin

plants.name: Carrot
plants.description: Cool-season root vegetable requiring deep loose soil for straight roots.
care_tips: Deep loose soil prevents forked roots
Thin seedlings to 5cm spacing
Excess nitrogen causes hairy roots and poor flavor

plants.name: Lettuce
plants.description: Fast-growing cool-season leafy vegetable, bolts in heat.
care_tips: Bolts (goes to seed) above 24°C — use shade cloth in summer
Succession plant every 2 weeks for continuous harvest
Shallow roots — water frequently but lightly

plants.name: Onion
plants.description: Bulb vegetable sensitive to day length, requires consistent moisture during bulbing.
care_tips: Stop watering when tops fall over — signals maturity
Day length triggers bulb formation
Sulfur deficiency reduces pungency and storage life

plants.name: Bell Pepper
plants.description: Warm-season vegetable requiring steady heat and consistent moisture.
care_tips: Blossom drop above 32°C or below 16°C
Calcium is critical — add gypsum if deficient
Stake plants when fruiting to prevent branch breakage

plants.name: Cucumber
plants.description: Fast-growing warm-season vine, high water requirement.
care_tips: Fruits are 95% water — never let soil dry out
Bitter cucumbers indicate water stress
Harvest frequently to encourage more fruit

plants.name: Spinach
plants.description: Fast cool-season leafy green, bolts quickly in heat.
care_tips: Bolts above 24°C — strictly a cool-season crop
High oxalate content reduced by cooking
Harvest outer leaves for continuous production

plants.name: Garlic
plants.description: Long-season bulb crop, planted in autumn and harvested in late spring.
care_tips: Requires cold vernalization period for bulb splitting
Stop watering when leaves yellow
Plant pointed end up, 5cm deep

plants.name: Eggplant / Aubergine
plants.description: Heat-loving vegetable, very suitable for hot climates like Iraq.
care_tips: Very heat tolerant — ideal for Basra summer
Bitter flavor indicates water or nutrient stress
Harvest before seeds harden for best quality

plants.name: Strawberry
plants.description: Low-growing perennial berry, produces best in cool conditions with high light.
care_tips: Remove runners to concentrate energy into fruit
Mulch to keep fruits clean and retain moisture
Day-neutral varieties produce year-round

plants.name: Watermelon
plants.description: Large warm-season vine fruit, excellent heat tolerance — perfect for Basra.
care_tips: Reduce irrigation 2 weeks before harvest to concentrate sugars
Hollow thump sound indicates ripeness
One strong vine produces better than multiple weak ones

plants.name: Grapes
plants.description: Perennial woody vine, requires dry conditions during ripening.
care_tips: High humidity causes fungal diseases — ensure good airflow
Deficit irrigation during veraison improves sugar concentration
Prune hard in dormancy for best fruit production

plants.name: Mango
plants.description: Tropical tree fruit, extremely heat tolerant and productive in Iraq's climate.
care_tips: Dry spell triggers flowering — withhold water for 4-6 weeks before desired bloom
Young trees need frost protection below 4°C
Deep taproot tolerates drought once established

plants.name: Banana
plants.description: Fast-growing tropical herb (not a tree), high water and nutrient demand.
care_tips: Pseudostem is 93% water — never let soil dry
Remove all but one sucker per plant
Wind damage causes cosmetic scarring on fruit — use windbreaks

plants.name: Lemon
plants.description: Evergreen citrus tree, everbearing fruit production in warm climates.
care_tips: Iron chlorosis in alkaline soils — chelated iron foliar spray
Deep infrequent irrigation better than shallow frequent
Fruit drop from sudden temperature changes — avoid stress

plants.name: Pomegranate
plants.description: Drought-tolerant deciduous shrub, thrives in Basra's hot dry climate.
care_tips: One of the most heat and drought tolerant fruits — ideal for Iraq
Irregular watering causes fruit cracking
Needs cold winters for good flowering — dormancy essential

plants.name: Date Palm
plants.description: Iraq's national tree —极 heat tolerant, the world's oldest cultivated fruit tree.
care_tips: Thrives where other trees fail — 'feet in water, head in fire'
Requires hand pollination for reliable fruit set
Salt tolerant — can use slightly brackish irrigation water

plants.name: Olive
plants.description: Ancient evergreen tree, extremely drought and heat tolerant once established.
care_tips: Established trees survive without irrigation — supplemental water increases yield
Biennial bearing — heavy crop one year, light next
Requires cold chilling hours below 10°C to flower

plants.name: Avocado
plants.description: Tropical/subtropical tree fruit, very sensitive to waterlogging and salt.
care_tips: Shallow feeder roots — mulch heavily and never cultivate
Chloride sensitive — avoid NaCl-rich water
Ethylene from ripening fruit triggers nearby trees to fruit

plants.name: Fig
plants.description: Ancient heat-tolerant deciduous fruit tree, thrives in Mediterranean and Middle Eastern climates.
care_tips: Very drought tolerant once established
Splitting fruit indicates irregular watering
Common figs don't need pollination — most home garden varieties

plants.name: Coconut Palm
plants.description: Tropical palm, productive year-round in humid coastal climates.
care_tips: Salt tolerant — thrives in coastal areas
Boron deficiency causes 'little leaf' disorder
Productive for 60-80 years with proper care

plants.name: Mint
plants.description: Vigorous spreading perennial herb, spreads aggressively via rhizomes.
care_tips: Contain in pots — spreads invasively in open beds
Harvest frequently to prevent flowering which reduces flavor
Partial shade acceptable — good for shaded garden spots

plants.name: Basil
plants.description: Warm-season aromatic herb, very sensitive to cold.
care_tips: Pinch off flower buds to extend leaf production
Chilling injury below 10°C causes black spotting
Companion plant with tomatoes — repels aphids

plants.name: Coriander / Cilantro
plants.description: Cool-season herb used extensively in Middle Eastern cuisine.
care_tips: Bolts rapidly in heat — sow in cooler months in Basra (Oct-Feb)
Direct sow — does not transplant well
Crush a seed before sowing to improve germination rate

plants.name: Turmeric
plants.description: Tropical rhizome spice requiring warm moist conditions — ideal for Iraq summers.
care_tips: Prefers filtered light — grows under taller plants
Harvest when lower leaves yellow
Cure rhizomes 1 week before storing

plants.name: Ginger
plants.description: Tropical rhizome with high culinary and medicinal value.
care_tips: Shade-tolerant — plant under taller crops
Rhizomes rot if waterlogged — excellent drainage essential
Divide rhizomes to each have at least one 'eye' before planting

plants.name: Saffron
plants.description: World's most expensive spice, harvested from flower stigmas. Requires hot dry summer dormancy.
care_tips: STOP watering completely June-August (hot dormancy triggers flowering)
Harvest stigmas within 24h of flower opening at dawn
Alkaline well-drained soil is essential — add lime if needed

plants.name: Sunflower
plants.description: Heat-tolerant oilseed crop, also grown for ornamental purposes.
care_tips: Deep taproot — drought tolerant after establishment
Heads track the sun only during vegetative stage
Birds eat seeds — net heads if growing for harvest

plants.name: Cotton
plants.description: Warm-season fiber crop, historically grown in Mesopotamia.
care_tips: Reduce irrigation after boll opening to aid drying
Heavy nitrogen at squaring stage increases yield
Dry weather during harvest prevents boll rot

plants.name: Sugar Cane
plants.description: Tropical perennial grass producing sucrose, grown in warm humid climates.
care_tips: Cool dry period before harvest increases sugar content
Ratoon crops from cut stumps for 3-5 cycles
High potassium demand — apply K fertilizer at grand growth stage

plants.name: Papaya
plants.description: Fast-growing tropical tree, fruits within one year, very productive in heat.
care_tips: Waterlogging for even 48h can be fatal — raised beds essential
Plant 3 seeds per hole, thin to best plant + one male
Fruits ripen from bottom of plant upward

plants.name: Pineapple
plants.description: Bromeliad that tolerates poor acidic soils, produces one fruit per plant.
care_tips: Absorbs water through leaves — spray foliage to water
Ethylene gas from ripe apple nearby triggers early fruiting
Ratoon from side shoots after main harvest

plants.name: Cantaloupe Melon
plants.description: Warm-season vine fruit, needs heat and dry conditions during ripening.
care_tips: Stop irrigation 1 week before harvest to concentrate sugar
Sweet fragrance at stem end indicates ripeness
One vine — two or three melons maximum for best size

plants.name: Peach
plants.description: Stone fruit tree originally from Persia (Iran/Iraq region), needs winter chilling.
care_tips: Requires 300-900 chilling hours below 7°C for fruit set
Thin fruits to one per 15cm for large peaches
Brown rot fungus thrives in humid conditions — avoid overhead watering

plants.name: Apple
plants.description: Temperate deciduous fruit tree, requires cold winters for dormancy.
care_tips: Most varieties need a cross-pollinator
June drop is natural — tree self-thins excess fruitlets
Calcium sprays prevent bitter pit disorder in stored fruit

plants.name: Orange
plants.description: Evergreen citrus tree, very productive in subtropical/Mediterranean climates.
care_tips: Deep infrequent watering encourages deep roots
Magnesium deficiency shows as yellow leaves with green veins
Frost below -3°C damages fruit — protect young trees
